title: Savory Indulgent Norwegian Cod Bisque
description: A rich, creamy, and comforting Norwegian-inspired dish featuring saffron and perfect for brunch or a late night supper. This recipe is a great way to warm up on a chilly day.
instructions: Melt 3 tablespoons of unsalted butter in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add 1 small onion, finely chopped, and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add 2 cloves of garlic, minced, and cook for 1 minute.
Add 1 cup of dry white wine and cook until reduced by half, stirring occasionally.
Add 1 cup of fish stock and 1 cup of heavy cream, and bring to a simmer.
Reduce heat to low and let cook, covered, for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in 1/2 teaspoon of saffron threads, soaked in 1 tablespoon of hot water.
Use an immersion blender to puree the bisque until smooth.
Return the saucepan to medium heat and add 1/2 cup of grated Gruyere cheese, stirring until melted and smooth.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh chives or parsley, if desired
